parts.label: Part 1
parts.instructions: Read the text and choose the correct answer (A, B or C).
parts.passage: More and more students today take on a part-time job while they study. For many, the main reason is simply money: rent, food and transport are expensive, and a small wage helps them stay independent. However, a job can offer more than just income. Working in a shop or a café teaches young people how to deal with customers, manage their time and work in a team — skills that look good on any CV later on. Of course, there are risks too. If students work too many hours, their grades may suffer and they may feel constantly tired. Career advisers therefore recommend a clear limit, usually no more than fifteen hours a week, and they remind students that their studies should always come first. A good balance, they say, is the key to getting the benefits without the stress.

items.question: Besides money, what is one benefit of a part-time job?

options.text: It guarantees a better final grade.
It teaches useful skills like teamwork and time management.
It replaces the need to study.
Working as many hours as possible.
Setting a clear limit and putting studies first.
Giving up studying to earn money.

items.question: Career advisers recommend working as many hours as possible.

parts.label: Part 1
parts.instructions: Read the transcript and choose the best answer.
parts.transcript:
Presenter: Today we're discussing bike-sharing schemes. Our guest, Maria, sold her car last year.
Maria: I save money and I don't waste time looking for parking. However, on rainy mornings I sometimes have to wait longer for a free bike.

options.text: She may wait longer for a bike on rainy mornings

parts.task: You read an article about plastic waste in the ocean. Write a letter to the editor explaining what worries you and suggesting practical actions.
Talk about a trip you have taken: where you went, how you travelled, what you enjoyed, and whether you would recommend it.
